title: The Scorpion and the Frog
narrative_elements: scorpion asks frog for a ride across stream
frog fears being stung but accepts reassurance
scorpion stings frog mid-crossing
both sink to their deaths

title: The Scorpion and the Ladybug
narrative_elements: scorpion befriends ladybug and promises safety
ladybug struggles to cross dangerous river
scorpion offers to carry her across
scorpion stings ladybug once safely across

title: The Serpent and the Eagle
narrative_elements: eagle seizes serpent in its talons
serpent coils around eagle in struggle
countryman frees eagle from serpent
serpent poisons countryman's drinking horn
eagle knocks horn from man's hand saving his life

title: The Serpent and the File
narrative_elements: serpent enters armorer's shop
serpent's skin is pricked by a file on the floor
serpent attacks file in rage
serpent cannot harm hard iron and gives up

title: The Shepherd and the Sea
narrative_elements: shepherd admires calm sea and desires to trade
shepherd sells flock and sets sail with cargo of dates
tempest threatens to sink ship
shepherd throws cargo overboard to survive
shepherd warns passerby sea is deceptively calm

title: The Shepherd's Boy and the Wolf
narrative_elements: shepherd boy cries wolf falsely multiple times
villagers come to help and are mocked
wolf finally comes for real
no one believes the boy's cries for help
wolf destroys the flock

title: The Shipwrecked Impostor
narrative_elements: chimpanzee clings to spar after shipwreck
dolphin rescues chimpanzee thinking it is a man
chimpanzee boasts and lies about knowing famous places
dolphin discovers the chimpanzee's lies
dolphin plunges chimpanzee into the sea

title: The Silkworm and Spider
narrative_elements: silkworm weaves silk for a royal order
spider sets up web nearby and boasts of speed
silkworm rebukes spider for making useless traps
silkworm explains her work adorns royalty

title: The Stag, the Wolf, and the Sheep
narrative_elements: stag asks sheep to lend wheat with wolf as surety
sheep suspects fraud from both stag and wolf
sheep refuses citing the unreliability of both

title: The Swallow and the Other Birds
narrative_elements: countryman sows hemp seeds in a field
swallow warns birds to eat every seed
birds ignore the swallow's warning
hemp grows and is made into nets
birds are caught in nets woven from the hemp

title: The Thief and the Housedog
narrative_elements: thief brings meat to silence the housedog
housedog rejects the bribe with suspicion
housedog reasons that sudden kindness signals danger
housedog vows to be more watchful

title: The Three Tradesmen
narrative_elements: city is besieged and inhabitants deliberate defense
bricklayer recommends bricks as best material
carpenter proposes timber as better defense
currier insists leather hides are superior

title: The Tortoise and the Birds
narrative_elements: tortoise asks eagle to carry it to new home
eagle agrees in exchange for reward
crow tells eagle tortoise is good eating
eagle drops tortoise on rocks at crow's suggestion
eagle and crow eat the tortoise

title: The Tortoise and the Eagle
narrative_elements: tortoise complains no one will teach her to fly
eagle offers to teach flying in exchange for riches
eagle carries tortoise nearly to clouds
eagle releases tortoise which crashes on mountain
tortoise laments exceeding her nature

title: The Traveler and Fortune
narrative_elements: traveler sleeps exhausted at the brink of a well
dame fortune wakes him before he falls in
fortune warns she will be blamed for man's own folly
fortune notes men impute calamities to her unjustly

title: The Traveler and His Dog
narrative_elements: traveler scolds dog for standing lazily at the door
traveler demands dog come immediately
dog reveals it is the traveler who was not ready

title: The Tree and the Reed
narrative_elements: tall tree mocks the reed for being small and timid
tree boasts nothing can uproot or bow it
hurricane uproots the tree
reed bends before the storm and stands upright after

title: The Trumpeter Taken Prisoner
narrative_elements: trumpeter is captured after leading soldiers to battle
trumpeter pleads innocence as he carries no weapon
enemy soldiers condemn him for inciting others to fight

title: The Two Pots
narrative_elements: river carries two pots downstream
earthen pot pleads brass pot keep its distance
earthen pot fears being shattered by the stronger brass pot

title: The Two Pots
narrative_elements: two pots float off down a river together
brass pot reassures earthen pot it will not strike it
earthen pot fears collision regardless of who strikes whom

title: The Two Travelers and the Axe
narrative_elements: one traveler finds an axe and claims it as his own
other traveler insists they should say 'we found it'
owner of axe pursues them
first traveler says 'we are undone'
second traveler insists he stick to his original selfish phrasing

title: The Vain Jackdaw
narrative_elements: jupiter announces contest for most beautiful bird to become king
jackdaw decorates himself with feathers dropped by other birds
birds expose jackdaw by stripping borrowed plumes
jackdaw is revealed as a plain jackdaw

title: The Vixen and the Lioness
narrative_elements: vixen taunts lioness for having only one cub
vixen boasts of her large litter
lioness calmly retorts that her one cub is a lion

title: The Widow and the Sheep
narrative_elements: poor widow shears sheep herself to avoid expense
widow uses shears so clumsily she cuts flesh along with fleece
sheep in pain advises widow to use a proper butcher or shearer

title: The Wolf and the Goat
narrative_elements: wolf spots goat feeding on steep precipice out of reach
wolf urges goat to come down claiming meadows below are better
goat sees through wolf's ruse and refuses

title: The Wolf and the Kid
narrative_elements: kid left on rooftop for safety spots a wolf below
kid jeers and taunts wolf from safe vantage
wolf points out it is the roof speaking not the kid's own bravery

title: The Wolf and the Shepherds
narrative_elements: wolf passes by and sees shepherds eating mutton
wolf remarks they would raise a clamor if he did the same

title: The Wolf in Sheep's Clothing
narrative_elements: wolf dons sheep's skin to infiltrate flock
wolf deceives shepherd and is shut in the fold at night
shepherd returns at night for meat and kills wolf by mistake

title: The Wolf in Sheep's Clothing
narrative_elements: wolf finds flayed sheep skin and puts it on
wolf strolls among the flock undetected
lamb follows wolf mistaking it for its mother
wolf leads lamb away and eats it

title: The Woodman and the Serpent
narrative_elements: woodman finds frozen serpent and warms it in his bosom
serpent revives by the fire at home
serpent raises its fangs to sting the woodman's child
woodman kills serpent with axe

title: The Young Thief and His Mother
narrative_elements: young man condemned to execution for theft
asks to see his mother before dying
bites off her ear as punishment for encouraging his early thieving
blames her for his fate

title: The Aethiop
narrative_elements: master believes servant's dark skin is merely dirt
master subjects servant to constant scrubbings
servant catches a cold but never changes color

title: The Abbot And The Flea
narrative_elements: abbot catches the flea that has been biting him
flea asks to confess sins before death
abbot holds flea in palm for confession
flea leaps away to freedom

title: The Lion And The Elephant
narrative_elements: lion complains to prometheus about fear of roosters
prometheus tells lion to accept what he was given
lion meets elephant who fears gnats
lion concludes he is better off than the elephant

title: The Donkey And The Wild Boar
narrative_elements: donkey greets wild boar as brother
boar indignantly rejects the kinship claim
donkey makes crude anatomical comparison
boar restrains himself to avoid soiling his honor

title: The Dog, The Sow And Aphrodite
narrative_elements: sow and dog argue viciously with each other
sow swears by aphrodite she will attack the dog
dog mocks sow noting aphrodite bans those who eat pork from her temple
sow retorts this proves the goddess loves her

title: The Camel And The Elephant
narrative_elements: animals consult on choosing a king
camel and elephant campaign on grounds of size and strength
ape declares camel unfit for lacking righteous anger
ape declares elephant unfit for fearing piglets

title: The Hunted Beaver
narrative_elements: beaver knows hunters prize a part of his body for medicine
when hard pressed by dogs beaver bites off the prized part himself
beaver throws it to hunters and escapes with his life

title: The Crow And The Water Jar
narrative_elements: thirsty crow finds a jar with water at the bottom
crow cannot reach the water and cannot tip the jar
crow drops pebbles into jar until water rises to the top
crow drinks and satisfies her thirst

title: The Shepherd And The Wolf Cubs
narrative_elements: shepherd raises wolf cubs hoping they will protect his flock
grown wolves first destroy shepherd's own flock
shepherd laments not killing them when they were young

title: Aesop And The Bow
narrative_elements: athenian man mocks aesop playing marbles with boys
aesop places unstrung bow in road as a riddle
man cannot explain the meaning
aesop explains a bow always strung will break; rest restores readiness

title: The Man Bitten By The Dog
narrative_elements: man is badly bitten by a dog
advisor tells him to feed bread soaked in his blood to the dog to heal the wound
man refuses fearing every dog in the city would then want to bite him

title: Aesop And The Hooligan
narrative_elements: hooligan strikes aesop with a stone
aesop gives the boy a coin and says that is all his money
aesop urges hooligan to hit the wealthy man coming by for a real reward
hooligan does so and is arrested and crucified

title: Aesop And The Shipbuilders
narrative_elements: shipbuilders taunt aesop and provoke him to speak
aesop tells them of chaos water and the earth swallowing the sea in three gulps
aesop warns that if the earth takes a third gulp all shipbuilders will be out of work

title: Aesop And The Soothsayers
narrative_elements: farmer's sheep give birth to lambs with human heads
soothsayers offer alarming and competing supernatural interpretations
aesop cuts through the nonsense suggesting the shepherds simply need wives

title: The Old Bull And The Young Bull
narrative_elements: father's son brutally whips slaves when unsupervised
aesop tells story of old bull yoked to wild young bull
farmer pairs them not for work but to curb the young bull's kicking
aesop advises father to keep unruly son beside him to restrain his recklessness

title: The Vegetables And The Weeds
narrative_elements: passerby asks why untended weeds thrive while cultivated plants wither
gardener explains wild plants are tended by divine providence
cultivated plants depend on human hands which are less reliable

title: Demades And The Athenians
narrative_elements: orator demades cannot get athenians to listen to his speech
he asks to tell an aesop's fable instead and they agree
he begins story of demeter swallow and eel reaching a river
he falls silent and rebukes them for preferring fables to politics

title: The Wolf, The Fox And The Monkey
narrative_elements: wolf accuses fox of theft
fox denies all charges
monkey judge declares wolf's claim unbelievable and fox guilty regardless of denial

title: The Crested Lark And The Farmer
narrative_elements: lark nests in ripened wheat field with chicks not yet able to fly
farmer asks friends then relatives to help harvest but they do not come
lark tells chicks not to worry while farmer relies on others
when farmer decides to harvest himself lark immediately moves her chicks

title: The Nut Tree And The People
narrative_elements: nut tree stands by the road laden with fruit
passers-by knock nuts off by throwing sticks and stones at the tree
tree laments that people enjoy its gifts but thank it with injury

title: The Bird Catcher And The Viper
narrative_elements: bird catcher extends reeds to catch thrush in a tree
while staring up he steps on a viper lying at his feet
viper bites him fatally
dying man laments he hunted another while he himself was hunted

title: The Magpie And Her Tail
narrative_elements: magpie's tailfeathers twitch uncontrollably no matter how she folds her wings
she flies far across the sea hoping a change of place will fix her habit
on the distant shore her tail twitches as before
magpie concludes changing place does not change habits

title: The Rose And The Amaranth
narrative_elements: amaranth praises the rose's beauty and fragrance
rose envies the amaranth's eternal bloom
rose notes it lives only briefly even if not plucked

title: The Clay Pot And The Rain
narrative_elements: winter storm drenches a potter's unfired clay pot left in a field
pot proudly declares its fine shape and name
storm warns of the coming deluge
floodwaters shatter the pot who dared to boast before the thunderclouds

title: The Donkey, The Priests And The Tambourines
narrative_elements: priests use donkey as beast of burden for their begging rounds
donkey dies from overwork and the whip
priests skin the donkey and make tambourines from his hide
priests joke that even in death the donkey keeps getting beaten

title: The Foxes At The River
narrative_elements: foxes gather at river wanting to drink but fear the current
one fox leaps in to show up her companions
current sweeps her away downstream
she shouts back she has a message to deliver and will show them later

title: The Shepherd And The Lion
narrative_elements: lion gets a thorn stuck in his paw and asks shepherd for help
shepherd removes the thorn and lion returns to the woods
shepherd is later falsely condemned and thrown to wild beasts
lion recognizes shepherd and places paw in his lap gently
king understands what happened and pardons the shepherd

title: Zeus And Prometheus
narrative_elements: prometheus fashions humans and animals at zeus's command
zeus finds animals far outnumber humans and orders some turned into people
those people retain animal souls inside human bodies

title: The Ant And The Dung Beetle
narrative_elements: ant labors all summer collecting grain for winter
dung beetle mocks ant for never resting
winter rains wash away the dung and the beetle goes hungry
ant refuses to share and rebukes beetle for mocking him while he worked

title: The Ant And The Cricket
narrative_elements: ant stores grain all summer while cricket sings
winter comes and cricket begs for food
ant asks what cricket did all summer
cricket says it was busy singing
ant tells cricket to dance through winter since it sang through summer

title: The Ant And The Fly
narrative_elements: fly boasts of dining at divine altars and kissing kings and queens
ant rebukes fly for being an uninvited pest at altars and shriving on manure
ant contrasts its own industrious winter stores with the fly's summer idleness
ant predicts fly will die in the cold while ant rests safe

title: The Ant, The Pigeon And The Bird-Catcher
narrative_elements: ant nearly drowns in a spring
pigeon drops a leaf for the ant to climb onto and survive
bird catcher arrives and sets limed reeds to trap the pigeon
ant bites the bird catcher's foot making him drop the reeds
pigeon flies to safety

title: Zeus And The Ant
narrative_elements: man steals from neighbours' crops despite farming his own land
zeus punishes him by turning him into an ant
even as an ant the creature continues gathering others' food

title: The Gods And Their Trees
narrative_elements: gods choose trees to adopt as their own
minerva asks why they chose fruitless trees
jupiter says they do not want to appear to expect reward for honor given
minerva chooses the olive for its useful fruit
jupiter praises minerva's practical wisdom

title: The Olive Tree And The Fig Tree
narrative_elements: olive tree mocks fig tree for losing leaves each season
snow falls and settles heavily on the leafy olive breaking its branches
snow passes through the bare fig tree harmlessly

title: The Cricket And The Owl
narrative_elements: cricket's noise prevents owl from sleeping during the day
owl asks cricket to keep quiet but cricket chirps louder
owl flatters cricket comparing its song to apollo's lyre
owl invites cricket to share nectar
cricket flies over and owl kills it

title: The Man And The Oracle
narrative_elements: man hides a sparrow in his cloak and goes to the oracle to test the god
man plans to contradict whatever answer apollo gives
apollo recognizes the man's evil intent
apollo tells the man the outcome is entirely in his own hands

title: Apollo And The Snake
narrative_elements: snake is repeatedly stepped on by many people
snake goes to apollo's temple to complain
apollo tells snake if it had killed the first person no one would have dared step on it again

title: The Black Man In The River
narrative_elements: someone sees a black man washing in a river
passerby jokes that stirring up mud will prevent him turning white

title: The Trees And The Bramble Bush
narrative_elements: pomegranate and apple tree argue at length about their beauty
bramble bush in the hedge overhears and tells them to stop quarreling

title: The Donkey Who Carried The God
narrative_elements: man leads donkey with a divine image on its back
people bow down as they pass
donkey thinks the bows are for him and begins to prance
donkey nearly throws off the sacred image
master beats donkey and reminds it the honor belongs to the god not the beast

title: The Bald Man And The Gardener
narrative_elements: bald man asks gardener for pumpkins
gardener insults and curses the bald man
bald man draws sword and seizes gardener by the hair
gardener hits bald man with a pumpkin
bald man overpowers and kills the gardener

title: The Monkey Elected King Of The Animals
narrative_elements: monkey dances and pleases all animals who elect him king
fox envies monkey and finds meat in a trap
fox leads monkey to the trap claiming it is treasure for the king
monkey grabs the bait and is caught in the trap
fox mocks monkey as unfit to be king

title: The Eagle And The Fox
narrative_elements: eagle steals fox's cubs and takes them to her nest
fox begs eagle to return her cubs but eagle refuses confident in her height
fox grabs a burning brand from an altar and threatens to burn the tree
eagle returns the cubs to avoid losing her own chicks in the flames

title: The Fox And The Cat
narrative_elements: fox boasts of seventeen tricks against dogs
cat knows only one trick: climbing trees
hunters and dogs give chase
cat escapes immediately by climbing a tree
dogs catch fox who is torn apart despite his bag of tricks

title: The Shepherd, The Wolf Cub And The Wolf
narrative_elements: shepherd raises wolf cub alongside dogs
grown wolf joins dogs in chasing outside wolves but secretly shares their prey
wolf secretly slaughters sheep when no outside wolf appears
shepherd discovers the truth and hangs the wolf

title: The Jackdaw And The Ravens
narrative_elements: large jackdaw scorns his own kind and joins the ravens
ravens do not recognize him and drive him away
jackdaw returns to jackdaws but they are angry about his presumption and reject him
jackdaw ends up belonging nowhere

title: The Rooster And The Pearl
narrative_elements: rooster finds a pearl in a dung heap while looking for food
rooster admires the pearl's beauty and laments it was he who found it
rooster concludes the pearl is no use to him and he would have preferred food

title: The Ax And The Trees
narrative_elements: man asks trees for hardest wood for an axe handle
trees provide wood from the wild olive
man fits the handle and begins chopping down trees
oak tells ash they deserve it for giving the enemy his weapon

title: The Crow And Athena
narrative_elements: crow sacrifices to athena and invites a dog to the feast
dog questions why crow wastes sacrifices on a goddess who hates crows
crow reasons this is all the more reason to sacrifice and hope to change athena's mind

title: The Shipwrecked Man And Athena
narrative_elements: wealthy athenian's ship capsizes in a storm
all others start swimming but athenian only prays to athena
a passing swimmer urges him to start moving his arms while praying

title: The Snake And The Crab
narrative_elements: crab and snake are friends and live together
crab urges snake to abandon its wicked ways but snake refuses
crab seizes sleeping snake and strangles it to death
dying snake stretches straight and crab remarks it should have been straightforward from the start

title: The Travellers And The Plane Tree
narrative_elements: travellers exhausted by summer heat rest in shade of plane tree
they remark the tree bears no fruit and is useless
tree interrupts and chides them for calling it useless while enjoying its shade

title: Socrates And His Friends
narrative_elements: socrates builds himself a very modest small house
neighbour asks why so great a man would build such a small house
socrates replies he only wishes he could fill it with true friends

title: The Monkey And The Dolphin
narrative_elements: monkey is brought on a sea voyage for company
ship capsizes in a storm and dolphin rescues monkey thinking it is a man
monkey lies about being an athenian nobleman and knowing piraeus
dolphin realizes monkey is lying and drowns it

title: Simonides And The Twin Gods
narrative_elements: simonides writes victory ode for a boxer devoting two thirds to the twin gods
boxer refuses to pay full fee telling simonides to collect from the gods
simonides is invited to dinner that evening
two mysterious young men summon simonides from the feast
banquet hall collapses killing everyone inside while simonides is spared

title: The Man, The Flea And Heracles
narrative_elements: flea jumps on a man's foot and sits there
man calls upon heracles for help defeating the flea
flea finally jumps off on its own
man laments that if heracles won't help with a flea he will not help against real enemies

title: The Boastful Athlete
narrative_elements: returned traveler boasts of making an incredible jump in rhodes witnessed by many
bystander tells him this is his rhodes and challenges him to jump right here and now
